Details: Chromium (VI) and chromium (III) removal by adsorption can be effective (up to 100 percent removal) and is strongly dependent on the adsorbent dose, influent pH, and initial concentration. Chromium (VI) adsorption is favorable in the acidic pH range for carbon and iron based sorbents. Iron based resins, carbon nanotubes, limestone, river bed sand are known to remove chromium (VI) under appropriate treatment conditions in lab scale experiments. Presence of organic compounds such as TCE can impede chromium (VI) removal by up to 40 percent.  One study found zeolite adsoprtive media effective in removig chromium (VI) from water at 30 minute empty bed contact time.
